questions.question: Explain the difference between admissible and consistent heuristics.
expectedPoints: Admissible heuristics never overestimate the true cost to goal
Consistent heuristics satisfy the triangle inequality: h(n) ≤ c(n, n') + h(n')
All consistent heuristics are admissible, but not all admissible heuristics are consistent
Consistency guarantees that A* will never re-expand nodes

questions.question: Compare and contrast Greedy Best-First Search and A* Search, highlighting their advantages and disadvantages.
expectedPoints: Greedy uses f(n) = h(n) while A* uses f(n) = g(n) + h(n)
Greedy is not optimal while A* is optimal with admissible heuristic
Greedy may not be complete while A* is complete
A* generally expands fewer nodes but requires more memory
Both use heuristic guidance but A* considers path cost already incurred
Practical applications where each is preferred
